findings.evidence: README.md:54 — reviewer field hardcoded to 'openai' in constraints. README.md:143 — REVIEWER_NAME = 'openai' constant in Phase 0. README.md:210 — 'REVIEWER_NAME = "openai" — always this string in the output JSON'. README.md:302-308 — Phase 3 concrete Python pseudocode for conditional expected_roles. README.md:247 — test asserts reviewer field is exactly 'openai' in all output paths.

README.md:55-57 — constraints mandate client.responses.create() and explain Chat Completions maintenance-mode status. README.md:183-193 — Phase 1 implementation shows client.responses.create() + response.output_text. README.md:341-342 — decision recorded.

README.md:50-53 — constraints mandate tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(dir=output_dir, delete=False) + os.replace(), shutil.move explicitly prohibited. README.md:106-111 — architecture block shows exact four-line atomic write sequence.

README.md:246 test implies strip ('Python strips and validates') but README.md:179-180 pre-flight pseudocode shows re.fullmatch(r'[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+', feature) with no .strip() call. A marker written by echo (standard tool) contains a trailing newline; \n fails the regex, so mode=plan always silently skips with a correctly-written-but-unstripped marker. The implementation pseudocode is the authoritative implementer reference and it is missing the strip. No explicit trailing-newline test case added.
README.md:334 — risk audit row documents accepted risk of full README content sent to API without token guard. Deferral explicitly noted. No further action required.

README.md:241 — 'Both calls invalid JSON → status=skipped, exit 2, failure logged'. README.md:242 — 'Auth error (mocked) → status=skipped, exit 2, failure logged'. README.md:243 — 'Truncated response → retry → still truncated → status=skipped, exit 2, failure logged'. All three previously broken test expectations now correctly specify silent-skip.

README.md:379 — Criterion 1 now specifies 'exits 2, JSON has status=skipped, reviewer="openai"'. README.md:380 — Criterion 1b added for mocked happy path: exits 0, status=ok, validates against review_schema.json.

Subsumed by W1. The core problem (missing explicit .strip() in implementation pseudocode) remains unresolved at the spec level. Test at line 246 implies strip happens but the pseudocode at lines 179-180 does not show it.

findings.finding: Risk audit table still references status=error for two LLM provider failure conditions: line 335 says 'model-not-found → error JSON, not crash' and line 337 says 'Results in status=error instead of status=skipped'. Both describe conditions that the canonical silent-skip policy (README.md:59-66, Phase 1 lines 203-208) now requires to produce status=skipped + exit 2. An implementer reading the risk audit after reading the constraint section encounters a direct contradiction. Line 337 is factually wrong about what correctly implemented code will do.
findings.suggested_fix: Line 335: change 'model-not-found → error JSON, not crash' to 'model-not-found → status=skipped, exit 2, failure logged to ~/.cache/tlmforge/llm_reviewer.log'. Line 337: change 'Results in status=error instead of status=skipped' to 'Results in status=skipped (key absent at call time → openai.AuthenticationError → silent skip per constraint)'. This eliminates the contradiction with README.md:59-66.

findings.finding: Implementation pseudocode for mode=plan marker pre-flight (line 179) shows re.fullmatch(r'[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+', feature) without an explicit .strip() call. Markers written by the standard shell echo command always contain a trailing newline; \n is not in the character class, so the regex always fails, causing mode=plan to always exit 2 with status=skipped even for valid marker content. This is a functional spec bug that will cause 100% silent skip rate on plan reviews in practice.
findings.suggested_fix: Change line 179 to read: 'feature = open(marker_path).read().strip()' then 're.fullmatch(r"[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+", feature)'. Add explicit test: 'marker file with trailing newline → strips correctly, proceeds to README lookup, exits 0 with mocked API'.
